content.content: Launching skill: code-review

content.text:
Review target: `763`

`high effort → 3+5 angles × 6 candidates → 1-vote verify (recall-biased) → ≤10 findings`

You are reviewing for **recall** at high effort: catch every real bug a careful
reviewer would catch in one sitting. At this level, catching real bugs matters
more than avoiding false positives. Err on the side of surfacing.

## Phase 0 — Gather the diff

Run `git diff @{upstream}...HEAD` (or `git diff main...HEAD` / `git diff HEAD~1`
if there's no upstream) to get the unified diff under review. If there are
uncommitted changes, or the range diff is empty, also run `git diff HEAD` and
include the working-tree changes in scope — the review often runs before the
commit. If a PR number, branch name, or file path was passed as an argument,
review that target instead. Treat this diff as the review scope.

## Phase 1 — Find candidates (3 correctness angles + 3 cleanup angles + 1 altitude angle + 1 conventions angle, up to 6 each)

Run **8 independent finder angles** via the Agent tool. Each
surfaces **up to 6 candidate findings** with `file`, `line`, a one-line
`summary`, and a concrete `failure_scenario`.

### Angle A — line-by-line diff scan

Read every hunk in the diff, line by line. Then Read the enclosing function for
each hunk — bugs in unchanged lines of a touched function are in scope (the PR
re-exposes or fails to fix them). For every line ask: what input, state, timing,
or platform makes this line wrong? Look for inverted/wrong conditions,
off-by-one, null/undefined deref, missing `await`, falsy-zero checks,
wrong-variable copy-paste, error swallowed in catch, unescaped regex metachars.

### Angle B — removed-behavior auditor

For every line the diff DELETES or replaces, name the invariant or behavior it
enforced, then search the new code for where that invariant is re-established.
If you can't find it, that's a candidate: a removed guard, a dropped error
path, a narrowed validation, a deleted test that was covering a real case.

### Angle C — cross-file tracer

For each function the diff changes, find its callers (Grep for the symbol) and
check whether the change breaks any call site: a new precondition, a changed
return shape, a new exception, a timing/ordering dependency. Also check callees:
does a parallel change in the same PR make a call unsafe?

### Reuse

The angles above hunt for bugs; this one and the next two hunt for cleanup in
the changed code. Flag new code that re-implements something the codebase
already has — Grep shared/utility modules and files adjacent to the change,
and name the existing helper to call instead.

### Simplification

Flag unnecessary complexity the diff adds: redundant or derivable state,
copy-paste with slight variation, deep nesting, dead code left behind. Name
the simpler form that does the same job.

### Efficiency

Flag wasted work the diff introduces: redundant computation or repeated I/O,
independent operations run sequentially, blocking work added to startup or
hot paths. Also flag long-lived objects built from closures or captured
environments — they keep the entire enclosing scope alive for the object's
lifetime (a memory leak when that scope holds large values); prefer a
class/struct that copies only the fields it needs. Name the cheaper
alternative.

### Altitude

Check that each change is implemented at the right depth, not as a fragile
bandaid. Special cases layered on shared infrastructure are a sign the fix
isn't deep enough — prefer generalizing the underlying mechanism over adding
special cases.

### Conventions (CLAUDE.md)

Find the CLAUDE.md files that govern the changed code: the user-level
~/.claude/CLAUDE.md, the repo-root CLAUDE.md, plus any CLAUDE.md or
CLAUDE.local.md in a directory that is an ancestor of a changed file (a
directory's CLAUDE.md only applies to files at or below it). Read each one
that exists, then check the diff for clear violations of the rules they state.

Only flag a violation when you can quote the exact rule and the exact line
that breaks it — no style preferences, no vague "spirit of the doc"
inferences. In the finding, name the CLAUDE.md path and quote the rule so the
report can cite it. If no CLAUDE.md applies, return nothing for this angle.

Cleanup, altitude, and conventions candidates use the same
`file`/`line`/`summary` shape; in `failure_scenario`, state the concrete
cost (what is duplicated, wasted, harder to maintain, or which CLAUDE.md rule
is broken) instead of a crash. Correctness bugs always outrank cleanup,
altitude, and conventions findings when the output cap forces a cut.

Pass every candidate with a nameable failure scenario through — finders that
silently drop half-believed candidates bypass the verify step and are the
dominant cause of misses.

## Phase 2 — Verify (1-vote, recall-biased)

Dedup near-duplicates (same defect, same location, same reason → keep one). For
each remaining candidate, run **one verifier** via the Agent tool:
give it the diff, the relevant file(s), and the candidate; it returns exactly
one of **CONFIRMED / PLAUSIBLE / REFUTED**.

**PLAUSIBLE by default** — do not refute a candidate for being "speculative" or
"depends on runtime state" when the state is realistic: concurrency races,
nil/undefined on a rare-but-reachable path (error handler, cold cache, missing
optional field), falsy-zero treated as missing, off-by-one on a boundary the
code does not exclude, retry storms / partial failures, regex/allowlist that
lost an anchor. These are PLAUSIBLE.

**REFUTED** only when constructible from the code: factually wrong (quote the
actual line); provably impossible (type/constant/invariant — show it); already
handled in this diff (cite the guard); or pure style with no observable effect.

Keep **CONFIRMED and PLAUSIBLE**. Drop REFUTED.

## Output

Return findings as a JSON array of at most 10 objects:

```json
[
  {
    "file": "path/to/file.ext",
    "line": 123,
    "summary": "one-sentence statement of the bug",
    "failure_scenario": "concrete inputs/state → wrong output/crash"
  }
]
```

Ranked most-severe first. If more than 10 survive, keep the 10 most
severe. If nothing survives verification, return `[]`.


## Posting to GitHub (--comment)

The `--comment` flag was passed. After producing the findings list, if the
review target is a GitHub PR, post each finding as an inline PR comment via
`mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment` (one call per finding;
include a suggestion block only when it fully fixes the issue). If that tool
is not available in this session, fall back to `gh api` (repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pr}/comments)
or print the findings instead. If the target is not a PR, print the findings
to the terminal and note that `--comment` was ignored.

content.text: All eight finders are done. Deduping gives ~7 distinct candidates. Most are mechanically verifiable from the files themselves; the one that needs real verification is the Unity Catalog namespace claim (finder A says `unitycatalog://` isn't in the OL spec — but my earlier naming research cited the spec and the Spark `UnityCatalogHandler` test asserting exactly that scheme). Spawning a verifier for that, and confirming the mechanical text claims directly:

input.prompt:
Verification task — return exactly one verdict: CONFIRMED / PLAUSIBLE / REFUTED, plus 3-5 sentences of evidence with URLs.

CANDIDATE FINDING under review: "docs/post-v1-assets-lineage-incidents-notes.md line 30 claims the OpenLineage dataset naming convention for Databricks Unity Catalog is `unitycatalog://{workspace_host}` + name `catalog.schema.table`, but that scheme is NOT part of the OpenLineage naming spec (which allegedly documents only `dbfs://{workspace}` for Databricks) — i.e., the doc invented a scheme, breaking its claim of adopting the spec verbatim."

Check the CURRENT canonical OpenLineage naming spec at https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenLineage/OpenLineage/main/website/docs/spec/naming.md (and/or https://openlineage.io/docs/spec/naming) for a Unity Catalog / Databricks row. Also check the Spark integration source https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenLineage/OpenLineage/main/integration/spark/spark40/src/main/java/io/openlineage/spark40/agent/lifecycle/plan/catalog/UnityCatalogHandler.java for what namespace it constructs.

Verdict rules: if the spec table or the reference implementation uses `unitycatalog://{host}` (with or without port) for UC datasets, the finding is REFUTED (the doc is correct). If the spec/implementation uses something else (dbfs://, databricks://), the finding is CONFIRMED. If the spec is silent but the Spark integration emits `unitycatalog://`, lean REFUTED but note the nuance. Final message = verdict + evidence only.

Verification task — return exactly one verdict: CONFIRMED / PLAUSIBLE / REFUTED, plus 3-5 sentences of evidence with URLs.

CANDIDATE FINDING under review: "docs/post-v1-assets-lineage-incidents-notes.md line 30 claims the OpenLineage dataset naming convention for Databricks Unity Catalog is `unitycatalog://{workspace_host}` + name `catalog.schema.table`, but that scheme is NOT part of the OpenLineage naming spec (which allegedly documents only `dbfs://{workspace}` for Databricks) — i.e., the doc invented a scheme, breaking its claim of adopting the spec verbatim."

Check the CURRENT canonical OpenLineage naming spec at https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenLineage/OpenLineage/main/website/docs/spec/naming.md (and/or https://openlineage.io/docs/spec/naming) for a Unity Catalog / Databricks row. Also check the Spark integration source https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenLineage/OpenLineage/main/integration/spark/spark40/src/main/java/io/openlineage/spark40/agent/lifecycle/plan/catalog/UnityCatalogHandler.java for what namespace it constructs.

Verdict rules: if the spec table or the reference implementation uses `unitycatalog://{host}` (with or without port) for UC datasets, the finding is REFUTED (the doc is correct). If the spec/implementation uses something else (dbfs://, databricks://), the finding is CONFIRMED. If the spec is silent but the Spark integration emits `unitycatalog://`, lean REFUTED but note the nuance. Final message = verdict + evidence only.

CLAUDE.md:286:**Next milestone:** **v1.1 Week 3 — Azure wind-down + local-first posture (due 2026-07-25).** **W2 CLOSED COMPLETE 2026-07-08** (milestone closed; exit gate MET 14/14; all 5 in-week follow-ups cleared — #571 (#699) run-detail checks_total graft, #640 (#700) flaky LiveRunProgress test, #643 (#701) stale-policy event, #605 (#702) redaction-safe run failure_reason, and **#286 Iceberg spike closed via ADR 0030** — engine-level read (Snowflake/UC iceberg tables) is free/zero-code, native `pyiceberg` v2 read proven green end-to-end, self-contained `iceberg` connection (Option A); native build → #716 (W3), Iceberg-v3 revisit → #717). W1 also redirected the dbt work (user decision): **#609 rescoped to self-hosted dbt Core** + **#611 filed — dbt as a third `OrchestrationProvider`** (webhook + artifact-poll, host-agnostic; dbt Cloud free tier has no API/scheduler, and Snowflake/Databricks hosting would couple observation to the vendor's run API), both W2. The **v1.1 cycle (6 weeks + a W7 stretch, 2026-07-04 → 2026-08-22) is planned** — week-level plan in [docs/progress.md](docs/progress.md) §Cycle plan; GitHub mirror = milestones `v1.1 Week 1..7` (W7 = stretch, due 2026-08-22) + cycle epic #597 + the **DataQ Roadmap** project (`v1.1 week` field; **65 issues scheduled** — #587–#596 filed at planning, then the full backlog remap 2026-07-04: `Backlog (post-v1 / testing)` renamed **`v1.1 Backlog`** and fully drained into W2–W7, so the backlog milestone is empty/default-for-new-filings; every scheduled issue carries an Acceptance-criteria checklist, every milestone its Exit gate). **Sequencing is subscription-driven:** the Snowflake subscription lapses within days (W1 front-loads the last-window live work — #194/#195/#587 — then retires the leg #588, with **PATs #461/ADR 0026 pulled forward right behind them** — breaks the Azure-AD-only auth dependency early in the Azure window; exit = 1 admin + 1 member PAT minted) and the Azure subscription ends ~2026-07-25 (W2 lands the portability seams verified against live Azure — OTel logs #524/#589, `SecretStore.delete` #372, dry-run depth #532 — and W3 ends in the deliberate wind-down #590 + local-first posture #591); W4–6 then run the roadmap's recommended sequence cloud-independently — `schema_drift` #592 → `anomaly` #593 + trend view #594 → scale-aware execution #595 (G-b) + the G-d incident/lineage design doc #596 → retro + `v1.1.0` tag. (Prior-week detail: Week 7 closed **COMPLETE 41/41** on 2026-07-03 (milestone + epic #176 closed; W7 exit gate — production-ready v1 deployed to Azure, CI/CD live, team onboarded — **met**). **Cloud deploy is DONE** (ADR 0024/0025 — app live on ACA; frontend cut over **SWA → Container App** per ADR 0028 §5, #509–#511). **Post-deploy hardening DONE** — Celery beat fix (#407, closes #405) + Key Vault credential fix (#408, closes #406) + image `:v7` redeploy (#409) — orchestration polling (ADF + Airflow), scheduled-suite dispatch, gap recovery, and periodic purge are all live. **Post-deploy feature batch DONE** (prod image now `:v10`, #438) — alerting publishers (Slack+email #413), redaction depth (#417), per-run outcome (#425), and **freshness/volume monitor-kinds end-to-end** (#426/#437, ADR 0012 Theme A pulled forward). **DONE this session:** the **FastMCP server (8 tools at `/mcp`)** (#460, ADR 0008), the **hardening/docs pass** (prod-docs gate #464, Swagger completeness + error-shape audit #465, deployment guide + env-var reference #468), **consistency hardening** (#456/#458, closes #308/#309), and the **visual-fidelity pass** (#459). **W7 close-out batch DONE (2026-07-01/02):** OTel spans #525 (+#524 follow-up) · vault-test #523 · #17 polish #522 · E2E expansion #526/#527 · live-smoke lane #531 · docs enrichment #528 (+#532 filed) · MCP-expansion candidates #530/#529 — **no in-repo Week-7 work remains**. **Live smoke + #492 DONE (2026-07-02):** Flows A/B/C green, spans verified, MCP protocol smoke passed, #492 fired→ingested in 4m14s (#534); fix batch #537/#538/#542 deployed (issues #535/#536/#539/#540). **Externals closed 2026-07-02:** team onboarding (six demo Entra users cross-shared at every ADR-0027 tier — no separate session, solo-dev) + KV purge-protection **decided left off** (`deploy/README.md`). **Final close 2026-07-03:** MCP client-config E2E passed (#550 — all 8 tools end-to-end via a real VS Code `.vscode/mcp.json` against live `/mcp/`; client setup guide at `docs/mcp-setup.md`). **All post-v1 / deferred work is consolidated in [context/post-v1-roadmap.md](context/post-v1-roadmap.md)** (incl. ADR 0026 — DataQ-issued API keys / service tokens, #461).)

<summary>Agent "Verify UC namespace spec claim" finished</summary>
<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>**Verdict: REFUTED**

The DataQ doc's line 30 (`unity_catalog` → namespace `unitycatalog://{workspace_host}`, name `catalog.schema.table`) matches the current canonical OpenLineage naming spec exactly. Fetching the live spec at https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenLineage/OpenLineage/main/website/docs/spec/naming.md shows a dedicated "Unity Catalog" row under the Catalog type category specifying `Namespace: unitycatalog://{host}` (or `unitycatalog://{host}:{port}`) with name format `{catalog}.{schema}.{table}` — this is distinct from the separate "Databricks File System (DBFS)" row (`dbfs://{workspace name}`), which the candidate finding wrongly claims is the only Databricks entry. This is corroborated by the reference Spark integration source at https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenLineage/OpenLineage/main/integration/spark/spark40/src/main/java/io/openlineage/spark40/agent/lifecycle/plan/catalog/UnityCatalogHandler.java, whose `unityCatalogNamespace()` method literally constructs `"unitycatalog://" + authority`, with an inline comment explaining the scheme/path are dropped "to maintain consistency with the other warehouse namespaces in the OpenLineage naming spec." The candidate finding's premise — that the spec documents only `dbfs://{workspace}` for Databricks and that `unitycatalog://` is an invented scheme — is factually wrong on both the spec table and the reference implementation.</result>

293:**`v1.1 Backlog` holds the G-d build set** — the #596 design doc (done in W3, 2026-07-10)
135:## Filed phase-1 issues (build order)
130:- **UI phasing:** (2) read-only Assets list + asset page (health across suites) →
19:   - **Parse dbt `manifest.json`** (fetched by the ADR-0029 3-scheme artifact reader) into a **`lineage_edges` cache** (`upstream_asset_id`, `downstream_asset_id`, `source`, `last_seen`) — a refreshed cache of external truth, not a graph we construct. Minimal stable field subset only (`parent_map`/`child_map` + node identity; never `compiled_code`), version-gated on `metadata.dbt_schema_version` (v12, stable dbt-core 1.8→1.11), ephemeral models collapsed, stream-parsed. This is the zero-infra blast-radius floor.
21:4. **Incident objects anchor to assets**: at most one open incident per `(asset_id, check_id)`, lifecycle `open → acknowledged → resolved`, occurrences instead of duplicates, auto-resolve-on-pass (per-suite configurable), the Theme-2 deterministic evidence card as payload, routed to the suite owner today / asset owner later. Alerts remain per-result notifications that reference the open incident.

cd /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ; gh pr comment 763 --body '## Agentic code review (high effort — 8 finder angles → 1-vote verify)

**8 findings survived verification** (7 posted inline). Two anchor outside the diff, recorded here:

1. **[correctness, `CLAUDE.md` §13 — not in diff]** Line ~286 still says "W4–6 then run … + the G-d incident/lineage design doc #596 → retro" and "the backlog milestone is empty/default-for-new-filings" — both stale once this PR lands (#596 done in W3; backlog holds the #757–#762 set among ~36 issues). CLAUDE.md is the assistant-facing source of truth, so the stale sequence line risks re-scoping already-shipped work. Same staleness in `docs/progress.md:26` ("Current cycle" Snapshot row, untouched by the diff).
2. **[consistency, GitHub issue #761 — not a repo file]** The design doc says #761 is "richer with #759/#760", but #761'\''s body only credits #759. Add the #760 (asset-view) enrichment mention to the issue body so implementers see the intended dependency.

**Notable refutation:** a finder claimed `unitycatalog://{workspace_host}` is not in the OpenLineage naming spec — **refuted** against the live spec (dedicated Unity Catalog row: `unitycatalog://{host}` / `{catalog}.{schema}.{table}`) and the Spark `UnityCatalogHandler` reference impl. The design doc is correct as written.

**Refuted/waived as house style:** the Snapshot-cell chronicling duplication, the W6 exit-gate annotation-instead-of-deletion, and the 6-issue list appearing in doc+ledger+roadmap (each copy links the canonical design doc).

All accepted findings will be fixed in a follow-up commit on this PR.'

[#651](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/651) author-time GX-registry validation of expectation-kind checks ([#686](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/686) + review follow-ups [#689](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/689)→[#690](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/690): dict-key size gap, bounded 422 echo, 1k→10k cap, PATCH no longer bricks pre-#651 legacy rows); header brand `nowrap`; live-verified 320/390/1280 + `SimpleList.test.tsx`). # ADR 0034 — Asset entity with OpenLineage identity; lineage is emitted/pulled, never built

- **Status:** Accepted
- **Date:** 2026-07-10
- **Deciders:** @TheurgicDuke771
- **Related:** ADR [0012](0012-monitor-kind-seam.md) (`metric_value` feeds the metrics facet), [0027](0027-suite-permission-model-workspace-admin.md) / [0033](0033-workspace-roles-rbac.md) (asset authz derives from the suite ladder; asset-metadata mutation is an Admin capability row), [0029](0029-dbt-orchestration-provider.md) (the artifact reader the manifest parser extends), [0031](0031-oss-byol-distribution-licensing.md) (rules out the OpenMetadata SDK)
- **Issue:** [#596](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/596) (gap G-d design). Full design: [docs/post-v1-assets-lineage-incidents-notes.md](../post-v1-assets-lineage-incidents-notes.md).

## Context

Gap G-d: DataQ has runs and alerts but no answer to "what broke downstream, who owns it, when was it resolved." Closing it needs lineage, incidents, an asset page, and (later) governance-catalog sync — and all four need the same missing primitive: today "the table" exists only implicitly inside `Suite.target` JSONB, so there is nothing for lineage edges, incidents, or catalog entities to reference. Separately, a lineage *source* already exists unconsumed: the ADR-0029 dbt provider polls `run_results.json`, and the sibling `manifest.json` (the model dependency graph) lands at the same artifacts URI every build.

## Decision

1. **A first-class `assets` table is the shared primitive, shipped first and alone.** Suites resolve their target to an `asset_id` on save; runs stamp it at dispatch; a backfill migration resolves existing targets. Suites remain the execution/authz grain (ADR 0027 untouched) — assets are the browse/reason grain. Two axes, like dbt models-vs-jobs.
2. **Asset identity = the OpenLineage dataset naming spec (`namespace` + `name`), adopted verbatim as the canonical key** — including its normalization rules (quote-strip, engine-returned case, the OL Snowflake account normalization) so our identifiers match `openlineage-dbt`/Spark emissions byte-for-byte, making future emission/pull interop a join instead of a mapping layer. Consequences accepted with it: DEV/QA accounts are *distinct* assets (cross-env grouping is a DataQ UI concern over the asset's `env` column, never an identity merge), and a flat-file pattern's asset is its literal base prefix (the Spark convention).
3. **Lineage is emitted and pulled, never authored.** Three slices, in order:
   - **Emit OpenLineage** from `run_service` via `openlineage-python` (Apache-2.0): START/COMPLETE/FAIL RunEvents with the target asset as input dataset carrying `DataQualityAssertionsDatasetFacet` (+ metrics facet for `metric_value` kinds). Dark by default (console transport, `OPENLINEAGE_DISABLED` honored); one emitter feeds Marquez/DataHub/Kafka with zero per-catalog code.
   - **Parse dbt `manifest.json`** (fetched by the ADR-0029 3-scheme artifact reader) into a **`lineage_edges` cache** (`upstream_asset_id`, `downstream_asset_id`, `source`, `last_seen`) — a refreshed cache of external truth, not a graph we construct. Minimal stable field subset only (`parent_map`/`child_map` + node identity; never `compiled_code`), version-gated on `metadata.dbt_schema_version` (v12, stable dbt-core 1.8→1.11), ephemeral models collapsed, stream-parsed. This is the zero-infra blast-radius floor.
   - **A `LineageProvider` seam** (mirrors `OrchestrationProvider`) for catalog pull, **Marquez as the reference impl** (purpose-built `GET /lineage` API, Apache-2.0, 2 containers on the compose stack; stalled release tagging accepted as low-risk for a dev-time reference consumer).
4. **Incident objects anchor to assets**: at most one open incident per `(asset_id, check_id)`, lifecycle `open → acknowledged → resolved`, occurrences instead of duplicates, auto-resolve-on-pass (per-suite configurable), the Theme-2 deterministic evidence card as payload, routed to the suite owner today / asset owner later. Alerts remain per-result notifications that reference the open incident.
5. **Asset/incident visibility is derived from suite grants, never separately granted** — visible iff the caller can `view` ≥1 composing suite, aggregation filtered to their grants, 404-no-leak preserved. Asset-metadata mutation (owner, description) starts workspace-Admin-only per the 0033 matrix pattern.

## Consequences

**Positive** — one additive migration unblocks four features (lineage, incidents, asset page, catalog sync); identity interop with the OL ecosystem is free forever; the dbt slice needs no new infrastructure and outlives Azure (`file://` artifacts); the emitter makes DataQ a good OL citizen before asking anything of the ecosystem; no authz re-keying, no graph engine to own.

**Negative / accepted** — OL naming makes cross-environment "same logical table" a two-asset reality the UI must group over; `lineage_edges` freshness is bounded by the artifact-poll cadence; Marquez's release cadence is slow; blast radius is only as complete as the sources feeding the cache (dbt first — warehouse-internal lineage like raw ADF copies won't appear until a catalog/OL source covers it); asset rows accrete as targets change (`last_seen` + a sweep, not deletes, is the cleanup posture).

## Alternatives considered

- **Build our own lineage graph** (the original v1-roadmap "React Flow, ~1 week" sketch) — rejected: authoring and maintaining graph truth is a product in itself; every serious catalog already knows it; pull/emit is cheaper and neutral (Theme 14).
- **Internal surrogate identity for assets** (own ID scheme, map to OL names later) — rejected: the mapping layer is permanent tax, and the OL spec already solves the hard cases (accounts, three-level names, file paths).
- **Asset-level ACLs** — rejected: re-keying authz off suites is a painful migration for little gain (ADR 0027's rationale); derivation keeps one authz source of truth.
- **DataHub as the reference consumer** — rejected for the compose stack (Kafka + OpenSearch + MySQL, 8 GB RAM minimum); it still works via the same emitter/seam when a user brings one.
- **OpenMetadata via its Python SDK** — rejected outright: `openmetadata-ingestion` is Collate source-available with a non-compete clause, prohibited by ADR 0031; REST-API-only integration would need its own ADR.
- **Purview / Atlas now** — parked: preview-grade OL support, Azure-only hosting, contra the wind-down posture.

content.content: Research: OpenLineage naming, dbt manifest, catalog integrations, repo doc conventions (4 agents)
Draft design doc: assets + lineage + incidents (docs/)
Draft ADR 0034 (asset entity + lineage pull) + index entry
File phase-1 build issues #757–#762
Update roadmap + progress.md cross-links / retarget #596 milestone W6→W3
Code review PR #763: 8 finder agents → verify → post inline comments
Fix accepted findings, merge PR, tick epic #597

content.text: Snapshot count (75) is still accurate. Now the two remaining fixes — CLAUDE.md's stale clauses and issue #761's body:

content.content: 286	**Next milestone:** **v1.1 Week 3 — Azure wind-down + local-first posture (due 2026-07-25).** **W2 CLOSED COMPLETE 2026-07-08** (milestone closed; exit gate MET 14/14; all 5 in-week follow-ups cleared — #571 (#699) run-detail checks_total graft, #640 (#700) flaky LiveRunProgress test, #643 (#701) stale-policy event, #605 (#702) redaction-safe run failure_reason, and **#286 Iceberg spike closed via ADR 0030** — engine-level read (Snowflake/UC iceberg tables) is free/zero-code, native `pyiceberg` v2 read proven green end-to-end, self-contained `iceberg` connection (Option A); native build → #716 (W3), Iceberg-v3 revisit → #717). W2 exit gate MET (observability + secrets + alerting vendor-neutral & Azure-verified, dry-run all-datasource): OTel logs #524/#589 · `SecretStore.delete` #372 + least-priv KV role #622 · dbt as a third `OrchestrationProvider` #609/#611 (ADR 0029) · alerting-hardening batch #386–#389/#416 · **#488** workspace-admin visibility in MCP tools + schedules ([#695](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/695)) · **#584** MCP NL tool-selection spot-check passed vs live `/mcp` (VS Code Copilot + W1 PAT, all 4 canonical queries correct) · **#532** dry-run preview extended to all datasources ([#697](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/697)). **W1 closed COMPLETE 2026-07-05** (milestone closed; exit gate MET): #194/#195 encrypted key-pair + GX kwargs migration live-verified (#602/#603) · #587 scale baseline captured (#607, docs/perf-baseline.md — extended to all datasources + renamed 2026-07-10) · #588 retirement rehearsed→REVERSED (trial actually runs to ~2026-07-25 — user correction; re-homed W3, #608/#610) · **#461 PATs phase 1 SHIPPED + LIVE** (#613, ADR 0026 Accepted: `dq_live_` sha256-at-rest behind the `get_current_user` seam, REST + `/mcp` identically; live exit met post-deploy — admin PAT `dq_live_NNZ5…` 30d + member PAT `dq_live_uTSi…` 90d exercised vs prod REST + `/mcp/`, 10-vs-4-suite / `/admin` 200-vs-403 authz matrix; **PATs are now the standing headless credential — az-CLI-bearer interim #565 retired**) · #583 MCP `profile_column` run-target default (#614). W1 also redirected the dbt work (user decision): **#609 rescoped to self-hosted dbt Core** + **#611 filed — dbt as a third `OrchestrationProvider`** (webhook + artifact-poll, host-agnostic; dbt Cloud free tier has no API/scheduler, and Snowflake/Databricks hosting would couple observation to the vendor's run API), both W2. The **v1.1 cycle (6 weeks + a W7 stretch, 2026-07-04 → 2026-08-22) is planned** — week-level plan in [docs/progress.md](docs/progress.md) §Cycle plan; GitHub mirror = milestones `v1.1 Week 1..7` (W7 = stretch, due 2026-08-22) + cycle epic #597 + the **DataQ Roadmap** project (`v1.1 week` field; **65 issues scheduled** — #587–#596 filed at planning, then the full backlog remap 2026-07-04: `Backlog (post-v1 / testing)` renamed **`v1.1 Backlog`** and fully drained into W2–W7, so the backlog milestone is empty/default-for-new-filings; every scheduled issue carries an Acceptance-criteria checklist, every milestone its Exit gate). **Sequencing is subscription-driven:** the Snowflake subscription lapses within days (W1 front-loads the last-window live work — #194/#195/#587 — then retires the leg #588, with **PATs #461/ADR 0026 pulled forward right behind them** — breaks the Azure-AD-only auth dependency early in the Azure window; exit = 1 admin + 1 member PAT minted) and the Azure subscription ends ~2026-07-25 (W2 lands the portability seams verified against live Azure — OTel logs #524/#589, `SecretStore.delete` #372, dry-run depth #532 — and W3 ends in the deliberate wind-down #590 + local-first posture #591); W4–6 then run the roadmap's recommended sequence cloud-independently — `schema_drift` #592 → `anomaly` #593 + trend view #594 → scale-aware execution #595 (G-b) + the G-d incident/lineage design doc #596 → retro + `v1.1.0` tag. (Prior-week detail: Week 7 closed **COMPLETE 41/41** on 2026-07-03 (milestone + epic #176 closed; W7 exit gate — production-ready v1 deployed to Azure, CI/CD live, team onboarded — **met**). **Cloud deploy is DONE** (ADR 0024/0025 — app live on ACA; frontend cut over **SWA → Container App** per ADR 0028 §5, #509–#511). **Post-deploy hardening DONE** — Celery beat fix (#407, closes #405) + Key Vault credential fix (#408, closes #406) + image `:v7` redeploy (#409) — orchestration polling (ADF + Airflow), scheduled-suite dispatch, gap recovery, and periodic purge are all live. **Post-deploy feature batch DONE** (prod image now `:v10`, #438) — alerting publishers (Slack+email #413), redaction depth (#417), per-run outcome (#425), and **freshness/volume monitor-kinds end-to-end** (#426/#437, ADR 0012 Theme A pulled forward). **DONE this session:** the **FastMCP server (8 tools at `/mcp`)** (#460, ADR 0008), the **hardening/docs pass** (prod-docs gate #464, Swagger completeness + error-shape audit #465, deployment guide + env-var reference #468), **consistency hardening** (#456/#458, closes #308/#309), and the **visual-fidelity pass** (#459). **W7 close-out batch DONE (2026-07-01/02):** OTel spans #525 (+#524 follow-up) · vault-test #523 · #17 polish #522 · E2E expansion #526/#527 · live-smoke lane #531 · docs enrichment #528 (+#532 filed) · MCP-expansion candidates #530/#529 — **no in-repo Week-7 work remains**. **Live smoke + #492 DONE (2026-07-02):** Flows A/B/C green, spans verified, MCP protocol smoke passed, #492 fired→ingested in 4m14s (#534); fix batch #537/#538/#542 deployed (issues #535/#536/#539/#540). **Externals closed 2026-07-02:** team onboarding (six demo Entra users cross-shared at every ADR-0027 tier — no separate session, solo-dev) + KV purge-protection **decided left off** (`deploy/README.md`). **Final close 2026-07-03:** MCP client-config E2E passed (#550 — all 8 tools end-to-end via a real VS Code `.vscode/mcp.json` against live `/mcp/`; client setup guide at `docs/mcp-setup.md`). **All post-v1 / deferred work is consolidated in [context/post-v1-roadmap.md](context/post-v1-roadmap.md)** (incl. ADR 0026 — DataQ-issued API keys / service tokens, #461).)

lines: - **Week 5 (complete, 18/18)** — execution engine + scheduling. Async run paths across all datasource types (Snowflake + flat-file batch #298 + UC #299); `error`/`skip` operational statuses (#297/#298, closes #122); run progress API #301 + **live run-progress drawer (A3)**; cancel run #302 (folds #227); **scheduling backend (A7)** — `schedules` table + `dispatch_due_schedules` 60s beat (DST-aware, no-backfill, `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`) + CRUD — and the **scheduled-runs UI (A6)**; **run-now panel**; ADF/Airflow 10-min polling #171 + **gap recovery #307** + provider-agnostic `GET /orchestration/pipelines` #305; **run-history retention sweep (A8)** (PII-minimisation, not a row delete — keeps `metric_value` trends). +re-tracked: check target-table #215, Suite Triggers UI #216, run-enablement read API PR-C0b. Closeout: #147/#317 merged, #327 filed. Recent-runs audit table moved to Week 6.

**Results surface (Phase C, done through PR-C1):** the in-app **`/results` page** shipped — runs table + run-detail drill-down (per-check results, severity tags) + orchestration pipeline-runs tab + sidebar nav (Connections · Suites · Results · Profile), on the C0b read API; ADR 0018 (in-app page over Grafana) accepted; demo seed lands runs/results/pipeline-runs. Rich dashboard widgets (health cards, trends, per-suite bars, export) and the redacted sample-row drill-down ([#226](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/226), closed by #365) shipped in Week 6.

**Admin control centre (#289, closes Week 4):** workspace-admin authz (config `WORKSPACE_ADMIN_EMAILS` allowlist — generic identity axis, no Azure/Entra claim read, no migration) + `admin_service` + `GET /admin/{suites,users,access}` (unscoped — bypasses owned-or-shared) behind `require_workspace_admin` (403); `/me` exposes `is_workspace_admin`; frontend `/admin` page (Suites · Users · Access) + `Forbidden` 403 + admin-only nav via a shared `MeProvider`/`useMe`. Pulled the Week-7 prototype-adoption admin tasks forward.

The **v1.1 cycle (6 weeks + a W7 stretch, 2026-07-04 → 2026-08-22) is planned** — week-level plan in [docs/progress.md](docs/progress.md) §Cycle plan; GitHub mirror = milestones `v1.1 Week 1..7` (W7 = stretch, due 2026-08-22) + cycle epic #597 + the **DataQ Roadmap** project (`v1.1 week` field; **65 issues scheduled** — #587–#596 filed at planning, then the full backlog remap 2026-07-04: `Backlog (post-v1 / testing)` renamed **`v1.1 Backlog`** and fully drained into W2–W7, leaving the backlog milestone as the default for new filings (it has since re-filled — incl. the G-d build set #757–#762, filed 2026-07-10); every scheduled issue carries an Acceptance-criteria checklist, every milestone its Exit gate). **Sequencing is subscription-driven:** the Snowflake subscription lapses within days (W1 front-loads the last-window live work — #194/#195/#587 — then retires the leg #588, with **PATs #461/ADR 0026 pulled forward right behind them** — breaks the Azure-AD-only auth dependency early in the Azure window; exit = 1 admin + 1 member PAT minted) and the Azure subscription ends ~2026-07-25 (W2 lands the portability seams verified against live Azure — OTel logs #524/#589, `SecretStore.delete` #372, dry-run depth #532 — and W3 ends in the deliberate wind-down #590 + local-first posture #591); W4–6 then run the roadmap's recommended sequence cloud-independently — `schema_drift` #592 → `anomaly` #593 + trend view #594 → scale-aware execution #595 (G-b) + the G-d incident/lineage design doc #596 → retro + `v1.1.0` tag. (Prior-week detail: Week 7 closed **COMPLETE 41/41** on 2026-07-03 (milestone + epic #176 closed; W7 exit gate — production-ready v1 deployed to Azure, CI/CD live, team onboarded — **met**). **Cloud deploy is DONE** (ADR 0024/0025 — app live on ACA; frontend cut over **SWA → Container App** per ADR 0028 §5, #509–#511). **Post-deploy hardening DONE** — Celery beat fix (#407, closes #405) + Key Vault credential fix (#408, closes #406) + image `:v7` redeploy (#409) — orchestration polling (ADF + Airflow), scheduled-suite dispatch, gap recovery, and periodic purge are all live. **Post-deploy feature batch DONE** (prod image now `:v10`, #438) — alerting publishers (Slack+email #413), redaction depth (#417), per-run outcome (#425), and **freshness/volume monitor-kinds end-to-end** (#426/#437, ADR 0012 Theme A pulled forward). **DONE this session:** the **FastMCP server (8 tools at `/mcp`)** (#460, ADR 0008), the **hardening/docs pass** (prod-docs gate #464, Swagger completeness + error-shape audit #465, deployment guide + env-var reference #468), **consistency hardening** (#456/#458, closes #308/#309), and the **visual-fidelity pass** (#459). **W7 close-out batch DONE (2026-07-01/02):** OTel spans #525 (+#524 follow-up) · vault-test #523 · #17 polish #522 · E2E expansion #526/#527 · live-smoke lane #531 · docs enrichment #528 (+#532 filed) · MCP-expansion candidates #530/#529 — **no in-repo Week-7 work remains**. **Live smoke + #492 DONE (2026-07-02):** Flows A/B/C green, spans verified, MCP protocol smoke passed, #492 fired→ingested in 4m14s (#534); fix batch #537/#538/#542 deployed (issues #535/#536/#539/#540). **Externals closed 2026-07-02:** team onboarding (six demo Entra users cross-shared at every ADR-0027 tier — no separate session, solo-dev) + KV purge-protection **decided left off** (`deploy/README.md`). **Final close 2026-07-03:** MCP client-config E2E passed (#550 — all 8 tools end-to-end via a real VS Code `.vscode/mcp.json` against live `/mcp/`; client setup guide at `docs/mcp-setup.md`). **All post-v1 / deferred work is consolidated in [context/post-v1-roadmap.md](context/post-v1-roadmap.md)** (incl. ADR 0026 — DataQ-issued API keys / service tokens, #461).)

**Active blockers:** none — v1 is shipped. Post-v1 follow-ups open by choice: spike survivors [#563](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/563), threshold-ordering [#568](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/568), checks_total edge [#571](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/571), CI flake [#573](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/573). The qa-verifier go-live workout (2026-07-03) also found and closed the one v1.0.0 blocker — NUL-byte input → unhandled 500 — same-day via [#570](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/570) (closed #567 + the older #371); non-blocking footguns from the same pass are filed as Backlog issues [#568](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/568)/[#571](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/571)/[#573](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/573). Open follow-ups (full register in [docs/progress.md](docs/progress.md); post-v1 backlog in [context/post-v1-roadmap.md](context/post-v1-roadmap.md)): profiler N+1 batching [#327](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/327); `SecretStore.delete` secret cleanup [#372](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/372) (filed in W6); Week-6 follow-ups [#349](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/349)/[#351](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/351) (#348 closed); Week-4 nits [#197](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/197)/[#199](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/199)/[#204](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/204); backend [#194](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/194)/[#195](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/195). See [docs/progress.md](docs/progress.md).

lines: - **Week 5 (complete, 18/18)** — execution engine + scheduling. Async run paths across all datasource types (Snowflake + flat-file batch #298 + UC #299); `error`/`skip` operational statuses (#297/#298, closes #122); run progress API #301 + **live run-progress drawer (A3)**; cancel run #302 (folds #227); **scheduling backend (A7)** — `schedules` table + `dispatch_due_schedules` 60s beat (DST-aware, no-backfill, `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`) + CRUD — and the **scheduled-runs UI (A6)**; **run-now panel**; ADF/Airflow 10-min polling #171 + **gap recovery #307** + provider-agnostic `GET /orchestration/pipelines` #305; **run-history retention sweep (A8)** (PII-minimisation, not a row delete — keeps `metric_value` trends). +re-tracked: check target-table #215, Suite Triggers UI #216, run-enablement read API PR-C0b. Closeout: #147/#317 merged, #327 filed. Recent-runs audit table moved to Week 6.

**Results surface (Phase C, done through PR-C1):** the in-app **`/results` page** shipped — runs table + run-detail drill-down (per-check results, severity tags) + orchestration pipeline-runs tab + sidebar nav (Connections · Suites · Results · Profile), on the C0b read API; ADR 0018 (in-app page over Grafana) accepted; demo seed lands runs/results/pipeline-runs. Rich dashboard widgets (health cards, trends, per-suite bars, export) and the redacted sample-row drill-down ([#226](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/226), closed by #365) shipped in Week 6.

**Admin control centre (#289, closes Week 4):** workspace-admin authz (config `WORKSPACE_ADMIN_EMAILS` allowlist — generic identity axis, no Azure/Entra claim read, no migration) + `admin_service` + `GET /admin/{suites,users,access}` (unscoped — bypasses owned-or-shared) behind `require_workspace_admin` (403); `/me` exposes `is_workspace_admin`; frontend `/admin` page (Suites · Users · Access) + `Forbidden` 403 + admin-only nav via a shared `MeProvider`/`useMe`. Pulled the Week-7 prototype-adoption admin tasks forward.

input.content:
---
name: project-gd-assets-lineage-design
description: G-d assets/lineage/incidents design DONE (#596→PR #763, ADR 0034); build issues #757–#762 in v1.1 Backlog; key decisions + the dbt/Marquez test-bed facts
metadata:
  type: project
---

**G-d (assets + lineage + incidents) design shipped 2026-07-10** — #596 pulled forward W6→W3, merged as PR #763: `docs/post-v1-assets-lineage-incidents-notes.md` + ADR 0034.

Load-bearing decisions (ADR 0034):
- **Asset identity = OpenLineage dataset naming, verbatim** (namespace+name; `unitycatalog://{host}` IS a real spec row — a review finder wrongly disputed it). DEV/QA = distinct assets by design; UI groups over `env`. Flat-file pattern asset = base prefix.
- **Lineage emitted/pulled, never built:** OL emission from run_service (dark by default) → dbt `manifest.json` → `lineage_edges` cache → `LineageProvider` seam (Marquez reference). **OpenMetadata SDK is license-blocked** (Collate source-available non-compete, ADR 0031); DataHub deferred (footprint); Purview parked.
- Incidents: one open per `(asset_id, check_id)`, reopen = NEW linked incident, evidence card payload, `ResultPublisher` delivery, suite-owner→asset-owner routing. Asset/incident authz derived from suite grants (no asset ACLs).

Build order (v1.1 Backlog, v1.2 generator input): #757 asset entity (prerequisite) → #758 OL emission · #759 dbt-manifest lineage → #760 asset view · #761 incidents · #762 LineageProvider/Marquez. Nav inversion files after #760.

Test-bed facts (verified against the real artifact): harness dbt project `dataq_lineage` = 4 `RETAIL` sources → 4 `ANALYTICS_STG` views → 2 `ANALYTICS` dynamic-table marts; manifest schema **v12 stable dbt-core 1.8→1.11**; artifacts at ADLS `raw/dbt/latest/` AND `file://`-capable (`upload_artifacts.py`), so the lineage path survives the Azure wind-down [[project-azure-winddown]]. Parse only `parent_map`/`child_map` + node identity, never `compiled_code`/`raw_code`; collapse ephemeral (null `relation_name`).

Related: [[project-v11-w3-state]], #310 (audit ADR, still W6, pairs with this).

content.content:
1	- [DataQ — Orchestration providers, not datasources](dataq_adf_is_orchestration.md) — ADF + Airflow share an OrchestrationProvider abstraction; monitor + detect failure + trigger suite on success only; never queryable datasources
2	- [DataQ — Working agreements](feedback_working_agreements.md) — single-functionality commits, manual test before next feature, GitHub issues for defects (no silent fixes), conda + Black for Python, locked coding structure
3	- [DataQ — v1.1 W3 hand-over](project_v11_w3_state.md) — ADRs 0031/0032/0033 merged; iceberg introspection #747 + live-found fixes #750/#752 deployed; **Flow D live** (harness `iceberg-writer` → `retail.purchase_orders`, suite `flow_d_iceberg` + Olivia share, `--iceberg` window leg); deploy incident #748 (zombie lock → PG restart); wind-down deferred ~2026-07-25
4	- [DataQ — /review before merge](feedback_review_before_merge.md) — spawn the /code-review AGENTS (never an inline self-review) on every PR; any deferred finding must be filed/noted, never silently dropped
5	- [DataQ — Verify locally before push](feedback_verify_locally_before_push.md) — run format/lint/typecheck/**bandit**/tests locally before commit; don't use CI as the first feedback loop (Ruff noqa ≠ Bandit B105)
6	- [DataQ — Fixup commits for in-review PRs](feedback_fixup_commits_in_review.md) — revise open PRs with a follow-up `fixup:` commit + plain push, never amend + force-push (squash-merge collapses it anyway)
7	- [DataQ — Test failure modes, not happy paths](feedback_test_failure_modes.md) — adversarial-input battery for data-ingesting code (backend/tests/support/adversarial.py); don't mock the seam under test; periodic mutmut spikes; ~94% line coverage still missed profiler 500s (#145/#147)
8	- [DataQ — GitHub hygiene: labels + milestones](feedback_issue_milestone.md) — every issue gets label(s) + milestone; every PR gets a milestone (feature-week; labels aren't a PR convention); closed-week milestones need the API-by-number set, not `gh pr edit`
9	- [DataQ — Local shell is Fish](dev-shell-is-fish.md) — Bash-tool commands run under Fish: quote glob flags (`--include="*.md"`), no `timeout` on macOS (use run_in_background), avoid `cd &&` chaining
10	- [DataQ — No creds in tracked files](feedback_no_creds_in_tracked_files.md) — never commit a credential (even mock/local); env templates ship blank, generate the local-dev password in setup.sh into the gitignored .env
11	- [DataQ — gh poisoned-cache 401](dev-gh-poisoned-cache-401.md) — `gh pr checks/merge` 401 "Bad credentials" while `gh api` works = stale entry in `~/.cache/gh`; rm the "Bad credentials" file, don't swap to REST
12	- [DataQ — review-thread resolution blocks merge](dev-review-thread-resolution-blocks-merge.md) — "base branch policy prohibits the merge" with all checks green = unresolved inline review threads (main ruleset); resolve via GraphQL resolveReviewThread, then auto-merge fires
13	- [DataQ — External harness env](project_harness_env.md) — ADR 0021 harness at `~/Coding/Python/DataQ-harness/` (not git-tracked); §1 terraform/ (plan-clean, unapplied) + §2 mockdata/ generator built; tracked by gitignored HARNESS_TODO.md
14	- [DataQ — Restart Container Apps after Postgres recreate](dev-aca-secret-restart-after-pg-recreate.md) — secret-injected env is a start-time snapshot; delete-recreate of shared PG → restart every dependent ACA revision or it resolves the old/dead host (caused the Airflow "Ooops!" 500)
15	- [DataQ — Live prod connections + demo users](project_live_connections_and_demo.md) — 5 live connections (snowflake/uc/adls/adf/airflow) with readable KV secret names; SF PAT (90d) + ADLS account SAS (exp 2027-06-28) to rotate; demo users + access on the "Retail Orders" suite; how to reach the prod DB from local
16	- [DataQ — Azure wind-down: harness stopped, wrap by ~2026-07-26](project_azure_winddown.md) — no PAYG upgrade; harness apps/ADF triggers/mockdata jobs stopped 2026-07-04 (burn CAD 21.7→4.5/day, credit 144.80 spent); wake runbook on #590; failing Airflow polls = expected noise

- [DataQ — Orchestration providers, not datasources](dataq_adf_is_orchestration.md) — ADF + Airflow share an OrchestrationProvider abstraction; monitor + detect failure + trigger suite on success only; never queryable datasources
- [DataQ — Working agreements](feedback_working_agreements.md) — single-functionality commits, manual test before next feature, GitHub issues for defects (no silent fixes), conda + Black for Python, locked coding structure
- [DataQ — v1.1 W3 hand-over](project_v11_w3_state.md) — ADRs 0031/0032/0033 merged; iceberg introspection #747 + live-found fixes #750/#752 deployed; **Flow D live** (harness `iceberg-writer` → `retail.purchase_orders`, suite `flow_d_iceberg` + Olivia share, `--iceberg` window leg); deploy incident #748 (zombie lock → PG restart); wind-down deferred ~2026-07-25
- [DataQ — /review before merge](feedback_review_before_merge.md) — spawn the /code-review AGENTS (never an inline self-review) on every PR; any deferred finding must be filed/noted, never silently dropped
- [DataQ — Verify locally before push](feedback_verify_locally_before_push.md) — run format/lint/typecheck/**bandit**/tests locally before commit; don't use CI as the first feedback loop (Ruff noqa ≠ Bandit B105)
- [DataQ — Fixup commits for in-review PRs](feedback_fixup_commits_in_review.md) — revise open PRs with a follow-up `fixup:` commit + plain push, never amend + force-push (squash-merge collapses it anyway)
- [DataQ — Test failure modes, not happy paths](feedback_test_failure_modes.md) — adversarial-input battery for data-ingesting code (backend/tests/support/adversarial.py); don't mock the seam under test; periodic mutmut spikes; ~94% line coverage still missed profiler 500s (#145/#147)
- [DataQ — GitHub hygiene: labels + milestones](feedback_issue_milestone.md) — every issue gets label(s) + milestone; every PR gets a milestone (feature-week; labels aren't a PR convention); closed-week milestones need the API-by-number set, not `gh pr edit`
- [DataQ — Local shell is Fish](dev-shell-is-fish.md) — Bash-tool commands run under Fish: quote glob flags (`--include="*.md"`), no `timeout` on macOS (use run_in_background), avoid `cd &&` chaining
- [DataQ — No creds in tracked files](feedback_no_creds_in_tracked_files.md) — never commit a credential (even mock/local); env templates ship blank, generate the local-dev password in setup.sh into the gitignored .env
- [DataQ — gh poisoned-cache 401](dev-gh-poisoned-cache-401.md) — `gh pr checks/merge` 401 "Bad credentials" while `gh api` works = stale entry in `~/.cache/gh`; rm the "Bad credentials" file, don't swap to REST
- [DataQ — review-thread resolution blocks merge](dev-review-thread-resolution-blocks-merge.md) — "base branch policy prohibits the merge" with all checks green = unresolved inline review threads (main ruleset); resolve via GraphQL resolveReviewThread, then auto-merge fires
- [DataQ — External harness env](project_harness_env.md) — ADR 0021 harness at `~/Coding/Python/DataQ-harness/` (not git-tracked); §1 terraform/ (plan-clean, unapplied) + §2 mockdata/ generator built; tracked by gitignored HARNESS_TODO.md
- [DataQ — Restart Container Apps after Postgres recreate](dev-aca-secret-restart-after-pg-recreate.md) — secret-injected env is a start-time snapshot; delete-recreate of shared PG → restart every dependent ACA revision or it resolves the old/dead host (caused the Airflow "Ooops!" 500)
- [DataQ — Live prod connections + demo users](project_live_connections_and_demo.md) — 5 live connections (snowflake/uc/adls/adf/airflow) with readable KV secret names; SF PAT (90d) + ADLS account SAS (exp 2027-06-28) to rotate; demo users + access on the "Retail Orders" suite; how to reach the prod DB from local
- [DataQ — Azure wind-down: harness stopped, wrap by ~2026-07-26](project_azure_winddown.md) — no PAYG upgrade; harness apps/ADF triggers/mockdata jobs stopped 2026-07-04 (burn CAD 21.7→4.5/day, credit 144.80 spent); wake runbook on #590; failing Airflow polls = expected noise
- [DataQ — dbt lineage harness project (#609)](project_dbt_lineage_harness.md) — self-hosted dbt Core in harness repo; RETAIL→ANALYTICS_STG views→ANALYTICS dynamic tables; runs as DATAQ_LOADER; change-tracking prereq; ACA job + ADLS artifacts for #611/#596
- [DataQ — shared Playwright session across subagents](feedback_shared_playwright_session.md) — main thread + subagents share one browser/tab; a backgrounded browser-driving subagent can silently clobber your tab mid-task
- [DataQ — secrets: inline-fetch only, never print/write](feedback_secret_fetch_inline_only.md) — KV secrets must go straight from command substitution into the consuming command; masked prints and chmod-600 scratch files are both blocked

content.text:
Done — the assets & data lineage piece is designed, decided, reviewed, and merged. Full recap:

## What shipped (PR [#763](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/763), squash-merged, closes #596)

- **Design doc** — [docs/post-v1-assets-lineage-incidents-notes.md](docs/post-v1-assets-lineage-incidents-notes.md): the asset entity, three lineage slices, incident objects, authz/UI phasing, and build order.
- **ADR 0034** — the decisions of record: asset identity = the **OpenLineage dataset-naming spec adopted verbatim** (byte-compatible with dbt-ol/Spark, so ecosystem interop is a join, not a mapping layer; DEV/QA = distinct assets by design); **lineage is emitted/pulled, never built** — OL emission with quality facets first (dark by default), the dbt `manifest.json` → `lineage_edges` cache as the zero-infra blast-radius floor, then a `LineageProvider` seam with Marquez as the reference consumer; incidents anchor to `(asset, check)` with the deterministic evidence card as payload; asset visibility derives from suite grants — no new ACL axis.
- **Six build issues filed** (#757–#762, `v1.1 Backlog`, each with AC checklists): asset entity → OL emission · dbt-manifest lineage → asset view · incidents · LineageProvider/Marquez.
- **Trackers reconciled**: #596 retargeted W6→W3 and ticked in epic #597; progress.md, roadmap Theme 3/14, ADR index, and CLAUDE.md §13 all updated.

Two research findings worth knowing: the **OpenMetadata Python SDK is license-blocked** (Collate source-available with a non-compete — ADR 0031 prohibits it; REST-only if ever), and our harness dbt project's real manifest (v12, stable across dbt-core 1.8→1.11) already gives the exact 10-node test graph, reachable via `file://` after the Azure wind-down.

The review ran as 8 finder agents + verify: 8 findings survived (all fixed in-PR — mostly stale "backlog is empty" claims and ADR↔doc drift), and one finder's claim that `unitycatalog://` isn't in the OL spec was **refuted** against the live spec, so the design stands as written.

**Natural next step when you're ready:** #757 (the asset entity migration) — it's the prerequisite for everything else, and starting it soon keeps the option of live-verifying the dbt/ADLS lineage slice inside the Azure window (~2026-07-25).

content.text:
Yes start with #757
One scenario to check after the lineage is build in DataQ update the lineage in data source, check if it is updating in DataQ. 
And another scenario, say tomorrow there is a new power bi report builds on top of any of the dataset DataQ monitoring, will DataQ be able to detect the PBI report as part of lineage?

lastPrompt: Yes start with #757 One scenario to check after the lineage is build in DataQ update the lineage in data source, check if it is updating in DataQ.  And another scenario, say tomorrow there is a new po…

content.text:
Starting #757 now. And both of your scenarios are exactly the right acceptance probes — quick answers up front, then I'll encode them into the issues and get building:

**Scenario 1 — lineage changes at the source propagate?** Yes, by design: `lineage_edges` is a *refreshed cache*, re-read from `manifest.json` on the dbt provider's poll (and we can refresh on the dbt webhook too, making it near-instant after a build). New edges upsert, removed edges age out via `last_seen`. I'll add this as an explicit end-to-end AC on #759.

**Scenario 2 — a new Power BI report on a monitored dataset?** Not via the first slices, honestly: the dbt manifest only knows dbt's own graph, and Power BI doesn't emit OpenLineage. PBI lineage lives in catalogs that ingest it (Purview natively, DataHub via its PowerBI source). That's precisely what the `LineageProvider` seam (#762) is for — a catalog impl that knows PBI would surface the report as a downstream node with zero changes to our model. I'll record this scenario on #762.

Kicking off the implementation survey and the issue updates in parallel:

input.prompt:
In /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend, gather everything needed to implement issue #757: a new `assets` table (OpenLineage namespace+name identity) + a resolver from suite target → asset + backfill + suite-save/run-dispatch hooks. Search breadth: thorough. Return a compact report with file:line refs and VERBATIM key snippets.

1. **Connection config shapes per type** — for `snowflake`, `unity_catalog`, `adls_gen2`, `s3`, `iceberg`: the exact config keys stored in `Connection.config` JSONB (account/host/database/schema/warehouse, container/account_url, bucket, catalog uri/warehouse). Look at `app/datasources/*.py` validate_config + any Pydantic spec models, and the connection specs the frontend drawer is driven by (`app/api/connections.py` or a specs module). I need: for snowflake — where the account identifier and default database/schema live; for UC — the workspace host key; for adls — account + container keys; s3 — bucket key; iceberg — catalog uri + warehouse keys.
2. **`resolve_target()`** in `app/services/run_target.py` — full signature, the `ResolvedTarget` dataclass fields, and how each connection type's target resolves (table/schema/catalog/path/pattern). Where is it called from (suite save? run dispatch? dry-run?).
3. **Suite save path** — `app/services/suite_service.py` (or wherever) create/update functions: where target/connection changes are validated (the place to hook asset resolution). Also the demo seed (where suites are seeded, for backfill verification).
4. **Run dispatch path** — where `Run` rows are created (run_service? celery task?) — the place to stamp `run.asset_id`.
5. **Migration precedents** — list the last 3 alembic versions (filenames + what they do); find one that did a DATA backfill (e.g. the check.kind default or the d2e3f4a5b6c7 suite_notifications one) and show how they structured it (op.execute SQL? Python loop with a table reflection?). Latest head revision id.
6. **Model + test patterns** — how a new model is added to `app/db/models.py` (naming/style), and where model/service tests live for a comparable feature (e.g. tests for suite target or schedules): file names + one example test function style. Also the adversarial harness location (tests/support/adversarial.py) usage example.
7. Anything that already normalizes Snowflake account identifiers or strips identifier quotes anywhere in the codebase (grep for 'account' normalization, 'strip.*quote', upper()).
Do not paste whole files — targeted excerpts only.

input.command: cd /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ; gh issue comment 759 --body '**Explicit refresh-propagation scenario (user-requested AC, 2026-07-10):** after lineage is first built, *change* the lineage at the source — edit a dbt model'"'"'s `ref()`/`source()` set (add an edge, remove an edge, add a model) and rebuild so a new `manifest.json` lands — and verify DataQ converges: new edges appear after the next poll (or webhook-triggered refresh — the dbt success event should trigger an immediate manifest re-read rather than waiting up to 10 min for the beat), removed edges age out via `last_seen` and stop appearing in blast-radius output. Test as a two-fixture sequence (manifest v1 → manifest v2) asserting the edge diff, plus the staleness cutoff.' >/dev/null && gh issue comment 762 --body '**BI-tool lineage scenario (user-requested, 2026-07-10) — e.g. a new Power BI report built on a monitored dataset:** neither the dbt manifest (#759) nor our own OL emission (#758) can see BI consumers — Power BI does not emit OpenLineage. Detecting "a PBI report now sits downstream of `ANALYTICS.mart_order_revenue`" requires pulling from a catalog that ingests PBI lineage: **Microsoft Purview** (native PBI lineage, Azure-only — parked per ADR 0034) or **DataHub** (has a PowerBI ingestion source; deferred to bring-your-own). This is exactly the seam-fit argument: a `LineageProvider` impl for such a catalog surfaces the report as a downstream asset node with no change to `lineage_edges` or the blast-radius query — BI nodes are assets whose identity maps through the provider (catalog URN → OL-style namespace/name), and they enrich incident blast radius ("3 PBI reports downstream"). **AC addition:** the seam contract must not assume downstream nodes are *tables* (node kind/type field on the pulled graph), so BI/dashboard nodes round-trip when a capable provider lands.' >/dev/null && gh api -X PATCH repos/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/757 -f milestone=12 -q '.milestone.title'

<result># Report: Implementing #757 (assets table + suite-target → asset resolver + backfill + hooks)

## 1. Connection config shapes per type (`app/datasources/*.py`)

**Snowflake** — `app/datasources/snowflake.py:57-91` (`SnowflakeConfig`):
```python
class SnowflakeConfig(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="forbid")
    account: str
    user: str
    database: str
    schema_: str = Field(alias="schema")
    warehouse: str
    role: str | None = None
    auth_type: Literal["password", "key_pair"] = "password"
```
Account identifier: `config.account` (raw string, e.g. `"ab12345.eu-west-1"` per `tests/api/test_suites.py:43`). No normalization anywhere (see item 7). Default database/schema live on the **connection**, not the target: `config.database` / `config.schema_` (aliased from JSON key `"schema"`). DSN built at `snowflake.py:157`: `f"snowflake://{credentials}@{config.account}/{config.database}/{config.schema_}?{query}"`.

**Unity Catalog** — `app/datasources/unity_catalog.py:36-63` (`UnityCatalogConfig`):
```python
class UnityCatalogConfig(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid")
    workspace_url: str
    warehouse_id: str
    @property
    def server_hostname(self) -> str:
        return urlparse(self.workspace_url).netloc
```
Workspace host key = `workspace_url` (e.g. `https://adb-1234.5.azuredatabricks.net`); the actual hostname is a derived `.server_hostname` property.

**ADLS Gen2** — `app/datasources/adls.py:32-44` (`AdlsConfig`):
```python
class AdlsConfig(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid")
    account_url: str
    container: str
    auth_type: Literal["sas", "managed_identity"] = "sas"
```
Account key = `account_url` (e.g. `https://<account>.blob.core.windows.net`); container key = `container`.

**S3** — `app/datasources/s3.py:32-46` (`S3Config`):
```python
class S3Config(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid")
    bucket: str
    region: str
    auth_type: Literal["access_key", "iam_role"] = "access_key"
    access_key_id: str | None = None
```
Bucket key = `bucket`.

**Iceberg** — `app/datasources/iceberg.py:66-91` (`IcebergConfig`):
```python
class IcebergConfig(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid")
    catalog_name: str = "default"
    catalog_type: IcebergCatalogType
    catalog_uri: str | None = None
    warehouse: str | None = None
    properties: dict[str, str] = {}
    secret_property: str | None = None
```
Catalog URI key = `catalog_uri` (required for `rest`/`sql`/`hive`, `iceberg.py:63` `_URI_REQUIRED`); warehouse/storage root key = `warehouse`.

## 2. `resolve_target()` — `app/services/run_target.py:82-137`

Full signature: `def resolve_target(conn_type: str, target: dict[str, Any] | None) -> ResolvedTarget`.

`ResolvedTarget` (`run_target.py:69-79`):
```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResolvedTarget:
    table: str
    schema: str | None
    catalog: str | None
    batch: BatchSpec | None = None
```
Per-type resolution inside `resolve_target`:
- `adls_gen2`/`s3` (flat-file, `_FLATFILE_TYPES` at `run_target.py:45`): `table` = `path` (or unresolved if `pattern`/batch); `schema`/`catalog` = `None`.
- `snowflake` (`run_target.py:112-114`): `table = _require(target, "table", ...)`, `schema = _str_or_none(target.get("schema"))`, `catalog = None`.
- `unity_catalog` (`run_target.py:116-121`): `table` + required `catalog = _require(target, "catalog", ...)`, `schema` optional.
- `iceberg` (`run_target.py:123-132`): folds optional `namespace` into `table` as `f"{namespace}.{table}"`; `schema`/`catalog` stay `None`.
- Anything else (orchestration providers) → raises `SuiteTargetInvalidError` (422).

Companions: `validate_target(conn_type, target)` (`run_target.py:140-147`, write-time wrapper, just calls `resolve_target` and discards result) and `materialize_path(...)` (`run_target.py:150-186`, run-time-only, resolves flat-file `BatchSpec` to a concrete path via `flatfile.resolve_batch_file`).

**Callers** (`grep` results):
- `app/services/suite_service.py:101, 165` — `create_suite`/`update_suite` call `validate_target` at save time.
- `app/api/v1/suites.py:249` — manual run-trigger endpoint calls `resolve_target` before queuing a `Run`.
- `app/worker/tasks.py:119, 140, 442` — schedule dispatch (`resolve_target` + `materialize_path`).
- `app/mcp/server.py:305, 412, 419` — MCP tool `trigger_suite_run` + a profiling tool.
- `app/services/dryrun_service.py:103, 144` — dry-run path.
- `app/alerting/builder.py:31-54` (`_target_label`) — deliberately **doesn't** call `resolve_target` (must never raise while building a report); instead hand-rolls a dotted `catalog.namespace.schema.table` label — useful precedent for an asset-name formatter that also must not throw.

## 3. Suite save path — `app/services/suite_service.py`

- `create_suite` (`suite_service.py:69-113`): connection-exists / not-orchestration-provider checks, then `if target is not None: run_target.validate_target(connection.type, target)` at **line 101**, before constructing the `Suite` row (line 102). **This is the hook point for resolving/upserting an `Asset` on create.**
- `update_suite` (`suite_service.py:142-170`): same call at **line 165**, guarded by `if target is not None`. Also the hook point for re-resolving asset on target change. `connection_id` is immutable (doc comment lines 4-7), so asset resolution never needs to handle a connection swap mid-suite.
- No demo-suite seeding lives in `suite_service.py` itself — seeding is in `scripts/seed_dev.py` and `app/services/probe.py`/`ensure_probe_fixtures` (used by `app/api/v1/probe.py:66`, `tests/services/test_probe_seed.py`). These are the fixtures to check post-backfill for asset linkage.

## 4. Run dispatch path — all 4 `Run(...)` creation sites

```
app/api/v1/suites.py:251   run = Run(suite_id=suite.id, status="queued", triggered_by=f"manual:{current_user.id}")
app/api/v1/probe.py:68     run = Run(suite_id=suite.id, status="queued", triggered_by=f"probe:{current_user.id}")
app/mcp/server.py:306      run = Run(suite_id=suite.id, status="queued", triggered_by=f"mcp:{user.id}")
app/worker/tasks.py:452    run = Run(suite_id=suite.id, status="queued", triggered_by=f"schedule:{schedule.id}")
```
All four sites resolve/validate the target via `run_target.resolve_target` (or catch `DataQError` for schedules, `worker/tasks.py:441-450`) *before* constructing `Run(...)` — each `resolve_target` call site is the natural place to also stamp `run.asset_id` (the resolved target + connection are already in scope there). `Run` model: `app/db/models.py:376-397`; no `asset_id` column yet.

## 5. Migration precedents

Head revision: **`e716a1b2c3d4`** (confirmed via `alembic heads`).

Chain (most recent 3, in order):
1. `e716a1b2c3d4_widen_connection_type_for_iceberg.py` — widens `ck_connections_type_valid` to add `'iceberg'`; pure CHECK-constraint swap via `op.execute("ALTER TABLE ... DROP/ADD CONSTRAINT ...")`, no data rewrite.
2. `c605d1e2f3a4_add_runs_failure_reason.py` — adds nullable `runs.failure_reason` (`op.add_column`, no backfill).
3. `d2e3f4a5b6c7_per_suite_slack_email_notifications.py` — adds two nullable columns to `suite_notifications` (no backfill).

**Data-backfill precedent**: `alembic/versions/9c59b6a44f33_week3_schema_seam_check_kind_severity_.py` (rev `9c59b6a44f33`, down_revision `aa33d80c2158`). Two backfill techniques shown verbatim:

a) NOT NULL column backfilled via `server_default` (no explicit UPDATE needed):
```python
op.add_column(
    "checks",
    sa.Column("kind", sa.String(32), nullable=False, server_default="expectation"),
)
op.create_check_constraint(
    "kind_valid", "checks",
    "kind IN ('expectation', 'freshness', 'volume', 'schema_drift', 'anomaly', 'comparison')",
)
```

b) Explicit `op.execute` UPDATE backfill of existing rows to a new vocabulary **before** swapping the CHECK constraint (so the constraint never rejects in-flight data):
```python
op.execute("UPDATE results SET status = 'pass' WHERE status = 'passed'")
op.execute("UPDATE results SET status = 'fail' WHERE status IN ('failed', 'skipped')")
op.drop_constraint("status_valid", "results", type_="check")
op.create_check_constraint(
    "status_valid", "results",
    "status IN ('pass', 'warn', 'fail', 'critical')",
)
```
No migration in this repo uses `sa.table()` reflection-loop-style backfill (row-by-row Python loop) — all data rewrites are raw `op.execute(SQL)`. Follow this style for the assets backfill (e.g. `INSERT INTO assets (...) SELECT DISTINCT connection_id, target->>'table', ... FROM suites WHERE target IS NOT NULL`).

ORM helper columns to reuse for the new `Asset` model (`app/db/models.py:85-108`):
```python
def _uuid_pk() -> Mapped[uuid.UUID]:
    return mapped_column(UUID(as_uuid=True), primary_key=True, server_default=text("gen_random_uuid()"))
def _created_at() -> Mapped[datetime]:
    return mapped_column(DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=False, server_default=func.now())
def _updated_at() -> Mapped[datetime]:
    return mapped_column(DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=False, server_default=func.now(), onupdate=func.now())
def _in_check(column: str, values: tuple[str, ...], name: str) -> CheckConstraint:
    ...
```

## 6. Test patterns

- Pure/unit style (no DB): `tests/services/test_run_target.py` — imports `resolve_target`/`validate_target`/`SuiteTargetInvalidError` directly, no fixtures. Example (verbatim, `test_run_target.py:24-26`):
```python
def test_snowflake_resolves_table_and_optional_schema() -> None:
    r = resolve_target("snowflake", {"table": "ORDERS", "schema": "SALES"})
    assert (r.table, r.schema, r.catalog) == ("ORDERS", "SALES", None)
```
- DB-backed style (real Postgres via TestClient): `tests/api/test_suites.py` (docstring at top: "Suite endpoint tests against a real Postgres (`db_session`) via TestClient. `get_db` is overridden to the shared test session..."). Helper `_connection(db_session)` at `test_suites.py:37-51` inserts a `Connection` row directly for the target-suite FK — a good template for asset-resolution tests that need a real `Connection.config`.
- Pure-fake-session style (no DB, hand-rolled fakes): `tests/services/test_run_service.py` uses `FakeSession`/`FakeRunner` (not `db_session`) — see `test_successful_run_persists_results_and_marks_succeeded` at line 189.

Comparable service dirs: `tests/services/test_run_dispatch.py`, `tests/services/test_run_target.py`, `tests/api/test_suites.py`. No `test_suite_service.py` exists — suite-service behavior (including the `validate_target` hook) is exercised through `tests/api/test_suites.py`.

Adversarial harness — `tests/support/adversarial.py`: exposes `ADVERSARIAL_FRAMES: list[tuple[str, pd.DataFrame]]` (parametrize fixture of hostile single-column DataFrames — mixed types, NaN/Inf, unicode, bytes, Arrow-backed variants) and `assert_json_safe(value: Any) -> None` (asserts `json.dumps(value, allow_nan=False)` succeeds). Two-line API: pass `ADVERSARIAL_FRAMES` into `pytest.mark.parametrize`, then call `assert_json_safe(...)` on any emitted value.

DB fixture: `tests/conftest.py` — `_db_engine` (session-scoped, `tests/conftest.py:169-198`, skips without `TEST_DATABASE_URL`/local Postgres, does `Base.metadata.create_all(engine)`) and `db_session` (`tests/conftest.py:201+`, function-scoped, uses `join_transaction_mode="create_savepoint"` so commits inside the test land on a savepoint that's rolled back after — tests never persist).

## 7. Existing Snowflake account normalization / quote-stripping

**None found.** Grep for `account.*strip|\.upper\(\)|unquote|normalize.*account|strip.*quote` across `app/` turned up only unrelated hits (`app/alerting/email.py:37` severity `.upper()`, `app/core/secrets.py:75` env-var-name `.upper()`, `app/services/column_classification.py:309` NULL-token `.upper()`, and comments in `app/datasources/monitors.py:54` / `app/services/profile_service.py:79,175` about Snowflake's *unquoted-identifier* charset, not account normalization). Snowflake `account` in `SnowflakeConfig` (`snowflake.py:66`) is stored and used completely raw — issue #757's asset-identity resolver will need to add its own normalization (e.g. case-folding/quote-stripping) from scratch; there's no existing helper to reuse.</result>
